session.user:
# /erk:plan-implement
Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.
This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:
1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan) 2. Executing the implementation 3. Running CI and submitting the PR
## Prerequisites
- Must be in a git repository managed by erk - GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated - One of: - A plan number, URL, or file path argument - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`) - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-implement # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan /erk:plan-implement 2521 # Fetch and implement plan #2521 /erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 # URL form /erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md # Implement from local markdown file ```
---
## CRITICAL: PR Operations
**NEVER** run direct GitHub CLI commands for PR operations during implementation:
- `gh pr edit` — destroys plan-header metadata block - `gh pr ready` — bypasses lifecycle tracking - `gh pr close` / `gh pr merge`
All PR operations are handled by `erk pr submit` in Step 12. It automatically:
- Generates the PR title and body - Preserves the plan-header metadata block - Marks draft PRs as ready for review - Updates lifecycle stage
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Set Up Implementation
Parse `` and run the consolidated setup command:
- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>` - **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)
```bash erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>] ```
This single command handles:
- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR) - Setting up from a local markdown file - Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup) - Creating/checking out the feature branch - Creating impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan content - Running impl-init validation - Cleaning up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push)
Otherwise, generate a branch slug and set up from the specified issue:
1. Fetch the title: `gh pr view <ISSUE_ARG> --json title -q .title` 2. Generate a branch slug from the title: - 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc" 3. Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <ISSUE_ARG> --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
If `setup-impl` exits with code 1 and `error: "no_plan_found"`, fall back to saving the current plan:
Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`
Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="aa084ce3-3580-4813-9c01-73c5dd119227" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
Parse the JSON output to get `plan_number`, then:
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <plan_number> ```
The `setup-impl` output includes `related_docs` (skills and docs to load) and `has_plan_tracking` (whether GitHub issue tracking is active).
If this fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context
Read `plan.md` from the impl directory set up in Step 1 to understand:
- Overall goal and context - Context & Understanding sections (API quirks, architectural insights, pitfalls) - Implementation phases and dependencies - Success criteria
**Context Consumption**: Plans contain expensive discoveries. Ignoring `[CRITICAL:]` tags, "Related Context:" subsections, or "DO NOT" items causes repeated mistakes.
### Step 3: Load Related Documentation
If plan contains "Related Documentation" section, load listed skills via Skill tool and read listed docs.
### Step 4: Create TodoWrite Entries
Create todo entries for each phase from the plan.
### Step 5: Signal GitHub Started
```bash erk exec impl-signal started --session-id="aa084ce3-3580-4813-9c01-73c5dd119227" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This also deletes the Claude plan file (from `~/.claude/plans/`) since:
- The content has been saved to GitHub - The content has been snapshotted to `.erk/scratch/` - Keeping it could cause confusion if the user tries to re-save
### Step 6: Execute Each Phase Sequentially
For each phase:
1. **Mark phase as in_progress** (in TodoWrite) 2. **Read task requirements** carefully 3. **Implement code AND tests together**: - Load `dignified-python-313` skill for coding standards - Load `fake-driven-testing` skill for test patterns - Follow project AGENTS.md standards 4. **Mark phase as completed** (in TodoWrite) 5. **Report progress**: changes made, what's next
**Important:** `plan.md` is immutable - NEVER edit during implementation
### Step 7: Report Progress
After each phase: report changes made and what's next.
### Step 8: Final Verification
Confirm all tasks executed, success criteria met, note deviations, summarize changes.
### Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended
```bash erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="aa084ce3-3580-4813-9c01-73c5dd119227" 2>/dev/null || true ```
### Step 10: Upload Session for Async Learn
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="aa084ce3-3580-4813-9c01-73c5dd119227" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.
### Step 11: Run CI Iteratively
1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions 2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands
**CRITICAL**: Never delete the impl directory - leave for user review (no auto-commit).
### Step 12: Submit PR
Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:
```bash erk pr submit ```
This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.
After successful submission, signal lifecycle transition:
```bash erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true ```
Then validate PR completion invariants:
```bash erk pr check --stage=impl ```
This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks (e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.
### Step 13: Output Format
- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..." - **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..." - **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes - **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"
---
## Related Commands
- `/erk:plan-save` - Save plan only, don't implement (for defer-to-later workflow) - `/erk:replan` - Re-plan an existing plan with current codebase state
session.thinking:
The user has invoked `/erk:plan-implement` without any arguments. Let me follow the instructions to set up implementation.
Since no argument was provided, I should run `erk exec setup-impl` which will auto-detect from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fail.
The current branch is `plnd/O8470-erk-objective-plan-8-03-01-2108`, which looks like a plan branch. Let me run the setup command.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Auto-detect and set up implementation from branch

session.tool_result:
1→# Add `modify_existing` input to one-shot.yml with conditional plan registration 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→This is node 1.2 of objective #8470 ("One-Shot Modification of Existing PRs"). Node 1.1 (PR #8472) added the `_dispatch_one_shot` handler in `launch_cmd.py` that dispatches the one-shot workflow with `modify_existing: "true"` in the workflow inputs. However, the `one-shot.yml` workflow currently has no `modify_existing` input defined — GitHub Actions silently ignores unknown inputs, so the flag is discarded. 6→ 7→This plan adds the `modify_existing` input to `one-shot.yml` and makes the plan registration step conditional on whether this is a new one-shot or a modification of an existing PR. 8→ 9→**Why this matters:** When modifying an existing PR, the "Register one-shot plan" step should be **skipped** because: 10→ 11→1. The plan entity (PR) already exists — no new plan registration is needed 12→2. The `register-one-shot-plan` command writes dispatch metadata and a "Queued for Implementation" comment, which are inappropriate for modifications to existing PRs 13→3. The lifecycle stage should not be reset to "planned" for an existing PR that may already be in a later stage 14→ 15→Similarly, the "Update objective roadmap node" step should be skipped in modify-existing mode since the existing PR already has its objective linkage. 16→ 17→## Changes 18→ 19→### 1. `.github/workflows/one-shot.yml` — Add `modify_existing` input and conditional steps 20→ 21→**Add new input** in the `workflow_dispatch.inputs` section, after `plan_only`: 22→ 23→```yaml 24→ modify_existing: 25→ description: "If true, modifying an existing PR (skip plan registration)" 26→ required: false 27→ type: string 28→ default: "" 29→``` 30→
... [110 lines omitted] ...
session.thinking:
This is a straightforward plan - it only modifies `.github/workflows/one-shot.yml` to: 1. Add a `modify_existing` input 2. Add a condition to skip plan registration when `modify_existing == 'true'` 3. Add a condition to skip objective roadmap update when `modify_existing == 'true'`
Let me read the workflow file to understand the current state.
session.assistant: Good, this is a focused YAML-only change. Let me read the workflow file and set up tasks.
session.tool_result:
1→name: one-shot 2→run-name: "one-shot:#${{ inputs.pr_number }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}" 3→ 4→on: 5→ workflow_dispatch: 6→ inputs: 7→ prompt: 8→ description: "Task description for autonomous execution" 9→ required: true 10→ type: string 11→ branch_name: 12→ description: "Branch to work on (created by CLI command)" 13→ required: true 14→ type: string 15→ pr_number: 16→ description: "Draft PR number (created by CLI command)" 17→ required: true 18→ type: string 19→ submitted_by: 20→ description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted" 21→ required: true 22→ type: string 23→ distinct_id: 24→ description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)" 25→ required: true 26→ type: string 27→ model_name: 28→ description: "Claude model to use for execution" 29→ required: false 30→ type: string
... [249 lines omitted] ...
82→ echo "::error title=Missing Secret::ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT secret is not configured or has expired. Add a PAT with 'repo' scope at: Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions" 164→ echo "::error::Planning succeeded but .erk/impl-context/plan.md not found" 168→ echo "::error::Planning succeeded but .erk/impl-context/plan-result.json not found" 192→ continue-on-error: true 241→ if git ls-files --error-unmatch .erk/impl-context/prompt.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then 244→ if git ls-files --error-unmatch .worker-impl/prompt.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then 258→ - name: Fail workflow if planning failed 261→ echo "One-shot planning failed with exit code: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.exit_code }}"
session.thinking:
Now I understand the file. Let me create the tasks and implement the changes. This is a straightforward YAML edit with 3 changes:
1. Add `modify_existing` input after `plan_only` (after line 56) 2. Update the "Register one-shot plan" step condition (line 191) 3. Update the "Update objective roadmap node" step condition (lines 204-208)
session.assistant: Clear plan with 3 edits to `one-shot.yml`. Let me create tasks and implement.

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Add new workflow_dispatch input after plan_only: modify_existing (string type, default empty, not required)

session.tool_result: Task #1 created successfully: Add `modify_existing` input to one-shot.yml

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Add condition to skip plan registration in modify-existing mode
Add `&& inputs.modify_existing != 'true'` to the "Register one-shot plan" step's if condition

session.tool_result: Task #2 created successfully: Add condition to skip plan registration in modify-existing mode

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Add condition to skip objective update in modify-existing mode
Add `&& inputs.modify_existing != 'true'` to the "Update objective roadmap node" step's if condition

session.tool_result: 52→ plan_only: 53→ description: "If true, only run the plan phase, skip implementation" 54→ required: false 55→ type: boolean 56→ default: false 57→ modify_existing: 58→ description: "If true, modifying an existing PR (skip plan registration)" 59→ required: false 60→ type: string 61→ default: "" 62→ 63→concurrency:

193→ erk pr check ${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }} 194→ 195→ - name: Register one-shot plan with issue and PR 196→ if: > 197→ steps.plan.outputs.plan_success == 'true' 198→ && steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id != '' 199→ && inputs.modify_existing != 'true' 200→ continue-on-error: true 201→ env: 202→ GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }} 203→ run: | 204→ erk exec register-one-shot-plan \ 205→ --plan-number "${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }}" \ 206→ --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \ 207→ --pr-number "${{ inputs.pr_number }}" \ 208→ --submitted-by "${{ inputs.submitted_by }}" \ 209→ --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}" 210→ 211→ - name: Update objective roadmap node 212→ if: > 213→ steps.plan.outputs.plan_success == 'true' 214→ && steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id != '' 215→ && inputs.objective_issue != '' 216→ && inputs.node_id != '' 217→ && inputs.modify_existing != 'true' 218→ env: 219→ GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }} 220→ OBJECTIVE_ISSUE: ${{ inputs.objective_issue }} 221→ NODE_ID: ${{ inputs.node_id }} 222→ PLAN_NUMBER: ${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }}
